sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Charles, a 38-year-old male, presented with a 2-year history of shortness of breath, which he describes as catching his breath a lot more than he used to.
He mentions that he considers himself to be pretty healthy but has noticed that his breathing has changed since moving to the city two years ago.
He reports that he gets shortness of breath suddenly, without any clear reason, and has had this symptom for an unknown duration.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of heavy drinking, which results in diarrhea after consuming alcohol.
He also has a dog that gets eye discharge, and he gets eye discharge himself, which he attributes to allergies.
He has never had any issues with allergies in the past.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his shortness of breath is his only current symptom.
He denies having any other symptoms such as a rash, nausea, vomiting, lip or throat swelling, or eye discharge when he is near his cat.
Current medications: The patient is currently using an albuterol inhaler prescribed by the urgent care for his shortness of breath.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's pulse is normal, and his pulse oximetry is normal.
He does not appear to be in any distress.
Physical exam: The patient does not have any rash, angioedema, or audible stridor.
He has faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
Test Results: The patient's chest X-ray and pulmonary function test were both normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Allergic asthma; Asthma symptoms; Normal chest X-ray; Normal pulmonary function test.
Plan:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler as before for asthma symptoms.
I also prescribed Singulair 10mg once daily to help decrease the occurrences of asthma.
I will proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing to target the triggers of his asthma.
If the skin testing does not identify the triggers, we will discuss further testing in his blood and consider immunotherapy.
Follow-up: I would like to see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
